_meta.description: Papers from Qatar-based institutions on transportation/infrastructure/mobility. Collected via web search 2026-05-12.
_meta.collection_method: Web search of QSpace (Qatar University repository), institutional pages, Google Scholar surface results, ScienceDirect, ResearchGate. Many full abstracts not directly accessible — first-500-char snippets are from search engine excerpts and may be paraphrased.
_meta.limitations: QSpace browsing pages only expose ~10 results per query; deeper coverage requires paid databases. HBKU and TAMU-Q research lists are heavily summary-driven on institutional websites and not in indexed repos.

papers.title: E-scooters in Qatar: Public perception, adoption intentions, and implications for urban mobility policy
papers.institution: Qatar University - Qatar Transportation and Traffic Safety Center
papers.abstract_snippet: Explores factors influencing e-scooter adoption in Qatar's car-dependent context with extreme climates
papers.key_finding: Identifies factors affecting e-scooter adoption intent in hot climate, car-dependent Qatar
papers.funding_source: Likely QU internal or QNRF (not confirmed in snippet)

papers.title: Professional vs. non-professional drivers: unveiling the risky driving behaviours on local road in Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: Compares driving behaviors between taxi drivers and other motorists in Qatar
papers.key_finding: Quantifies risk-behavior differences between professional and non-professional drivers

papers.title: Can pedestrians safely navigate shared sidewalks with e-scooters and bicycles?
papers.abstract_snippet: Analyzes pedestrian safety in mixed micro-mobility environments using surrogate safety measures
papers.key_finding: Identifies pedestrian risk factors in shared sidewalk environments

papers.title: Visual attention and driving behavior of male autistic individuals while encountering driving hazards
papers.abstract_snippet: Uses driving simulator to examine hazard perception in specialized populations
papers.key_finding: Quantifies hazard perception differences

papers.title: Examining access to daily functions through the lens of the 15-minute city concept
papers.abstract_snippet: Evaluates urban accessibility and proximity-based service planning in dense cities
papers.key_finding: Assesses degree to which Doha neighborhoods meet 15-minute city accessibility

papers.title: Safety and Operational Performance of Signalized Roundabouts: A Case Study in Doha
papers.abstract_snippet: INACCESSIBLE
papers.methodology: Case study + operational/safety analysis at signalized roundabouts
papers.key_finding: Operational and safety evaluation of signalized roundabout conversions in Doha

papers.title: Motorized Transportation and the UHI Effect in Doha: The Impact of Traffic on the Heat Island Effect
papers.abstract_snippet: Research reveals that automobile traffic is a key contributor to heat formation in Doha, especially in traffic jams.
papers.key_finding: Traffic congestion contributes measurably to urban heat island effect in Doha

papers.title: Concept Design of Major Roads and Infrastructure in the Center of Doha City: Phase 4 project
papers.abstract_snippet: Concept design for C-Ring Road, D-Ring Road, and various streets in Doha center.
papers.key_finding: Engineering concept design (gray literature, not research)

papers.title: Multimodal Transport for Smart Mobility in Emerging Cities: Case of Doha (Msheireb Downtown)
authors: Qatar University - Architecture and Urban Planning
papers.abstract_snippet: Explores multimodal transport in Msheireb and impacts of autonomous vehicles.
papers.key_finding: Frames Msheireb as showcase for smart mobility integration

papers.title: Area-Specific Traffic Peak Hour Timing Using Traffic Signal Detectors
papers.key_finding: Doha exhibits three peaks per day, different from typical two-peak Western pattern

papers.title: Analysis of mode choice effects from the introduction of Doha Metro using machine learning and statistical analysis
papers.abstract_snippet: Discrete choice binary logit, multinomial logit, XGBoost on revealed/stated preference surveys.
papers.key_finding: Quantifies factors driving mode-shift to Doha Metro

papers.title: Assessment of Service Quality and Trust of E-Public Transportation in Doha Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: Despite advanced automation, Doha Metro has <30% adoption among economically active. Affordability and dynamic pricing could increase ridership.
papers.key_finding: Trust + affordability are bigger drivers of ridership than service quality alone

papers.title: An integrated urban-transport smart growth model around metro stations: A case of Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: Regression model showing land-use type substantially impacts ridership; mixed-use development recommended.
papers.key_finding: Mixed land use increases metro ridership

papers.title: Qatar residents' satisfaction for using the Doha Metro Rail System: An analysis for sustainable transportation
papers.abstract_snippet: Structural equation model with 9 variables affecting trust/satisfaction.
papers.key_finding: Empathy and tangibles among strongest predictors of satisfaction

papers.title: Advancing urban mobility in the State of Qatar — Establishing a framework for autonomous vehicles in Doha
papers.abstract_snippet: AV implementation in Doha not thoroughly analyzed; recommends integrated planning of transport, land use, and digital systems.
papers.key_finding: Proposes AV-readiness framework for Doha

papers.title: Sustainable Transportation in Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: INACCESSIBLE (ResearchGate gated)
papers.key_finding: Survey of sustainable transport policies and initiatives

papers.title: Transit-Oriented Development in Middle Eastern Cities: The Urban Renewal Framework for the Souq Waqif in Doha, Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: Examines urban sprawl, congestion, walkability, inefficient transit network in Doha. Funded as QU Student Grant 2022 cycle.
papers.key_finding: Proposes TOD-based renewal framework for Souq Waqif area

papers.title: Analysis of road traffic crashes in the State of Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: Crash data analysis with focus on causality patterns.
papers.key_finding: Identifies crash patterns and contributing factors in Qatar

papers.title: Traffic safety culture of professional drivers in the State of Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: IATSS Research publication on professional driver safety culture.
papers.key_finding: Profiles safety culture of professional drivers in Qatar

papers.title: Road safety status during COVID-19 pandemic: exploring public and road safety expert's opinions
papers.abstract_snippet: Expert opinion analysis on COVID-period road safety changes.
papers.key_finding: Pandemic-period changes in driving and safety

papers.title: Multilevel resilience assessment framework: evaluating Qatar road network resilience for FIFA WC 2022
authors: HBKU CSE Sustainable Built Environment Group
papers.abstract_snippet: Resilience under intentional attacks, accidents, and extreme flooding scenarios. Metro network performed well; road concerns under flooding.
papers.key_finding: Road network resilient to attacks/accidents but vulnerable to extreme flooding

papers.title: Traffic air pollution emissions in Qatar: Actual, projections and mitigation
papers.institution: Hamad Bin Khalifa University (Division of Engineering Mgmt / ELMI)
papers.abstract_snippet: Book chapter; analyzes actual emissions, projects future scenarios, evaluates electrification mitigation.
papers.key_finding: Light-duty vehicles dominate emissions; 5% car / 25% bus electrification yields ~10% CO2 reduction

papers.title: Low Transportation Emission Analysis and Projection Using LEAP: The Case of Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: First use of LEAP tool for Qatar transportation emissions. Light-duty vehicles dominate.
papers.key_finding: Quantifies emissions reduction potential under different policy scenarios

papers.title: Evaluation of particulate matter emissions from non-passenger diesel vehicles in Qatar
papers.abstract_snippet: First evaluation of PM emissions from all registered diesel heavy-duty vehicles in Qatar.
papers.key_finding: Quantifies PM contribution from diesel HDVs; ~170k diesel vehicles projected by 2030 without policy

papers.title: Speed cameras and motor vehicle injury fatalities in Qatar (2000-2010)
papers.abstract_snippet: Pre-camera period (2000-2006) mean death rate 19.9 per 100k. Post-camera (2007-2010) 14.7 per 100k.
papers.key_finding: Speed cameras associated with significant reduction in fatal MV injuries

papers.title: Motor vehicle injuries in Qatar: time trends in a rapidly developing Middle Eastern nation
papers.abstract_snippet: Time trend analysis of MV injuries in Qatar's development period.
papers.key_finding: Identifies trend reversals in injuries during rapid urbanization

papers.title: Applying the five-pillar matrix to the decade of action for road safety in Qatar: identifying gaps and priorities
papers.abstract_snippet: Gap analysis using WHO five-pillar framework for road safety in Qatar.
papers.key_finding: Identifies priority gaps in Qatar's road safety implementation

papers.title: Sustainable Mobility Symposium (UDST CeSMO)
authors: UDST Centre of Excellence for Sustainable Mobility
papers.institution: University of Doha for Science and Technology
papers.abstract_snippet: Symposium covered fuel-integrated battery thermal management, dhow electrification, marine charging infrastructure, lightweight automotive materials, supply chain/maritime defense, waste-to-mobility.
papers.key_finding: UDST is focused on vehicle technology, marine electrification, not urban-network analysis

papers.title: Toward Sustainable Urban Development in Doha: Implementation of Transit Villages through a Multiple-Layer Analysis
papers.abstract_snippet: Multiple-layer analysis of transit village implementation in Doha.
papers.key_finding: Recommends transit village approach to TOD around metro stations

papers.title: Urban Sustainability and Livability: An Analysis of Doha's Urban-form and Possible Mitigation Strategies
papers.abstract_snippet: Doha urban form analysis; sprawl, SUV dependence, lack of public transport context.
papers.key_finding: Doha sprawl driven by privacy/villa preference; mitigation strategies proposed
